tests.test_name: JUnit - PO - single test, core output structure
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a single JUnit test file receives a valid, fully-resolved JSON output containing at least one named test case.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded output are populated — no placeholders or missing data remain
At least one test case is detected and documented from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test case is identifiable by name

tests.test_name: JUnit - PO - Parameterized expands one test per row
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a data-driven JUnit test file sees each parameter row expanded into its own separate test case in the output.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a parameterized JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded output are fully populated with no gaps
Each data row from the parameterized test is treated as a distinct test case, resulting in multiple entries

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each expanded test case is individually named

tests.test_name: JUnit - QAM - single test, coverage matrix populated
tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a single JUnit test file receives output that includes a populated test coverage matrix alongside standard test documentation.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded output are fully populated with no gaps
At least one test case is detected and documented from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test case is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: First test has a non-empty test_coverage_matrix array
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The QA Manager view includes a coverage matrix with at least one entry for the test case

tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a data-driven JUnit test file sees the parameterized test collapsed into a single test case whose coverage matrix rows represent each data example using the correct structure.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a parameterized JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded output are fully populated with no gaps
All parameter rows are collapsed into a single test case entry rather than expanded individually

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The collapsed test case is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix row count matches the examples
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each data row from the parameterized test is represented as a row in the coverage matrix

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix rows use the correct schema for generic
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each coverage matrix row conforms to the expected generic format with all required fields present
